redFlags.title: Filing ITR-1 When a Director in Any Company
redFlags.description: A taxpayer who is or was a director in any company at any time during FY 2025-26 CANNOT use ITR-1. This includes directors of startups, family companies, or any listed/unlisted company — regardless of whether they received remuneration.
redFlags.consequence: Return treated as defective. Notice u/s 139(9) issued. Return deemed invalid if not rectified within 15 days.
redFlags.howToDetect: Check Form DIR-12 status or MCA portal. If PAN is linked to any company as director, you cannot use ITR-1.

redFlags.title: Filing ITR-1 Despite Holding Unlisted Equity Shares
redFlags.description: Any person who held equity shares of an unlisted company at any point during FY 2025-26 (even for one day) cannot file ITR-1. This includes ESOP shares before they are listed, shares of private limited companies inherited, or held as collateral.

redFlags.title: Filing ITR-1 with Capital Gains (Even Exempt LTCG)
redFlags.description: A common misconception: since LTCG u/s 112A up to ₹1.25L is exempt, some taxpayers believe ITR-1 can be used. This is WRONG. ANY capital gain — even fully exempt — must be disclosed in ITR-2.
redFlags.consequence: Non-disclosure of capital gains is detected via AIS (MF/equity transaction data from exchanges). Department issues notice for concealment.
redFlags.correction: File ITR-2 if any capital gain/loss occurred during the year

redFlags.title: Salary Income Mismatch with Form 16 / Form 26AS
redFlags.description: If the salary income declared in ITR-1 does not match the employer-reported salary in Form 26AS/AIS, the return will be flagged during processing.
redFlags.consequence: Demand notice from CPC (Centralized Processing Centre). Tax credit of TDS may be disallowed if gross salary doesn't match.
redFlags.howToDetect: Always cross-check: Form 16 (Part A) = AIS/26AS. Any difference means employer may have filed incorrect TDS return.
redFlags.correction: Reconcile with employer before filing. If mismatch persists, declare correct income and file grievance with employer to correct TDS return.

redFlags.title: Not Reporting Savings Bank / FD Interest
redFlags.description: Banks report all interest payments above ₹10,000 to AIS. Even smaller amounts appear in AIS. Omitting bank interest — even ₹500 — creates a mismatch between AIS and ITR that triggers notices.
redFlags.consequence: Notice u/s 143(1)(a) proposing adjustment for difference between AIS and ITR. If persistent, may be treated as concealment.
redFlags.howToDetect: Download AIS from income tax portal before filing and reconcile all entries.
redFlags.correction: Always download AIS and match all interest entries. Report all interest income in Schedule OS.

redFlags.title: Not Reporting Dividend Income
redFlags.description: Post DDT abolition (FY 2020-21 onwards), all dividend income is taxable. Dividend data from companies and mutual funds is reported in AIS. Non-disclosure triggers mismatch.
redFlags.consequence: Tax demand for undisclosed dividend + interest u/s 234A/234B
redFlags.correction: Include all dividend income in Schedule OS. Cross-check with AIS.

redFlags.title: Claiming Old Regime Deductions Under New Tax Regime
redFlags.description: A very common mistake for FY 2025-26: taxpayers file under the new regime but still claim 80C (PF, LIC), 80D (health insurance), HRA exemption u/s 10(13A), or home loan interest on self-occupied property. These are NOT available under new regime.
redFlags.consequence: Return will be processed with incorrect deductions. Department may issue demand notice adjusting for incorrect deductions.
redFlags.correction: Under new regime, available deductions are: standard deduction ₹75,000, employer NPS u/s 80CCD(2), 80CCH. Nothing else. If old regime deductions are needed, switch to old regime.

redFlags.title: Claiming Deduction u/s 80C for Cash Investments
redFlags.description: Certain 80C instruments require non-cash payment: ELSS MF purchases must be via banking channel; LIC premium above ₹2,000 should be by cheque/digital. Cash investments may not be eligible.
redFlags.consequence: Deduction disallowed during scrutiny if cash payments are found.
redFlags.correction: Ensure 80C investments are made via banking channels. Keep investment receipts.

redFlags.title: Not E-Verifying ITR Within 30 Days
redFlags.description: From August 2022, the ITR must be e-verified within 30 days of filing. Non-verification within 30 days causes the ITR to be treated as not filed — resulting in late filing, penalties, and no refund processing.
redFlags.consequence: ITR treated as invalid. Refunds withheld. Penalties for late filing u/s 234F. Late filing interest u/s 234A.
redFlags.correction: E-verify immediately after filing using Aadhaar OTP, net banking EVC, or DSC.
redFlags.legalReference: Rule 12AC, Income-tax Rules 1962; CBDT Notification No. 5/2023

redFlags.title: Claiming Home Loan Interest on Self-Occupied Property Under New Regime
redFlags.description: Under the new tax regime (default for FY 2025-26), interest on housing loan for self-occupied property u/s 24(b) is NOT deductible. This creates income/loss from self-occupied property.
redFlags.consequence: If claimed in error, ITR processed with wrong tax liability leading to demand.
redFlags.correction: Only let-out property loan interest is deductible under both regimes. Self-occupied interest deduction requires opting for old regime.

redFlags.title: Not Paying Self-Assessment Tax Before Filing
redFlags.description: If tax liability after TDS credit is positive, self-assessment tax must be paid before or at the time of filing. Filing ITR-1 without paying outstanding tax makes the return defective.
redFlags.consequence: Defective return notice. Interest u/s 234B/234C on unpaid tax.
redFlags.correction: Pay self-assessment tax via Challan 280 (online) before filing. Enter challan details in Schedule IT.

redFlags.title: Significant Increase in Income Compared to Previous Year Without Explanation
redFlags.description: While not a disqualifying factor, a sudden large increase in income (say, 300%) compared to prior years triggers risk-based selection for scrutiny assessment.
redFlags.consequence: May be selected for scrutiny under Section 143(2). Taxpayer must explain income increase with documents.
redFlags.howToAvoid: Maintain documentation for income spikes (salary increase letter, business sale documents, property sale documents, etc.)

validationErrors.errorMessage: Total Income shown in Part B does not match sum of income from all schedules
validationErrors.cause: Arithmetic error or inconsistency between schedule entries and Part B
validationErrors.resolution: Recalculate: Salary + HP + Other Sources = GTI. Verify each schedule total flows to Part B correctly.

validationErrors.errorMessage: TDS amount claimed exceeds TDS shown in Form 26AS
validationErrors.cause: Claiming TDS that was not deposited by deductor
validationErrors.resolution: Only claim TDS visible in Form 26AS / AIS. If employer TDS is missing, contact employer.

validationErrors.errorMessage: PAN and Aadhaar not linked — return cannot be processed
validationErrors.cause: PAN-Aadhaar linking deadline has passed and penalty applies
validationErrors.resolution: Complete PAN-Aadhaar linking on income tax portal before filing. Pay penalty if applicable.

validationErrors.errorMessage: Bank IFSC code invalid or branch closed
validationErrors.cause: Outdated IFSC after bank merger (e.g., OBC/UBI merged into PNB)
validationErrors.resolution: Verify current IFSC from the bank. Update bank account details in pre-filing section.

validationErrors.errorMessage: Self-assessment tax challan not matching CIN/BSR/Date combination
validationErrors.resolution: Re-enter BSR code, challan serial number, and date from the physical Challan 280 or online payment receipt.
